Party_Details.Name: Wong Sung Boon

Facts.Text: Wong Sung Boon was appointed as Senior Managing Director and CEO of Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd.
Wong's appointment as Senior MD was governed by a Letter of Appointment dated 1 April 2016.
Wong was issued a termination notice summarily dismissing him from his position.
FXS suspended Wong with full pay pending the outcome of an audit investigation.
Deloitte issued a report with findings on the audit investigation.
FXS entered into a Master Services Agreement with I-Connect Interactive Pte Ltd for a satellite infrastructure project.
FXS entered into a second Master Services Agreement with I-Connect for a fiber optic infrastructure project.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the summary dismissal of Wong Sung Boon was justified under the terms of his employment contract.
Party_Details.Rule: An employer may summarily dismiss an employee if the employee's conduct amounts to gross misconduct or if there is a serious contravention of the employment contract.
Party_Details.Application: The Plaintiff argued that the dismissal was without cause and not in accordance with the employment contract. The Defendants claimed that the dismissal was justified due to Wong's actions causing financial loss and exposure to liability.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that while there were breaches of internal procedures, they were subsequently rectified and did not justify summary dismissal.

Party_Details.Name: Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd appointed Wong Sung Boon as Senior Managing Director.
Entered into a Master Services Agreement with I-Connect Interactive Pte Ltd.
Entered into a second Master Services Agreement with I-Connect for a fiber optic infrastructure project.
Commissioned Deloitte to conduct an audit investigation.
Issued a termination notice to Wong Sung Boon summarily dismissing him.
Deloitte issued a report with findings on the audit investigation.
Suspended Wong with full pay pending the outcome of the audit investigation.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd was legally responsible for disputed dismissal of Wong Sung Boon.
Party_Details.Rule: An employer must provide just cause for summary dismissal, failing which the dismissal may be deemed disputed.
Party_Details.Application: The Defendants argued that Wong's actions justified summary dismissal due to financial losses and exposure to liability. The Plaintiff contended that the dismissal was without proper cause and procedure.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court determined that while procedural breaches occurred, they were rectified and did not justify summary dismissal.

Party_Details.Name: Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Entered into a Master Services Agreement with I-Connect Interactive Pte Ltd.
Entered into a second Master Services Agreement with I-Connect for a fiber optic infrastructure project.
Commissioned Deloitte to conduct an audit investigation.
Issued a termination notice to Wong Sung Boon summarily dismissing him.
Deloitte issued a report with findings on the audit investigation.
Suspended Wong with full pay pending the outcome of the audit investigation.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Wong Sung Boon is legally responsible for financial losses incurred by Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act with reasonable diligence and in the best interest of the company to avoid liability for financial losses.
Party_Details.Application: The Plaintiff by counterclaim argued that Wong's actions led to financial losses and exposure to liability. Wong contended that he acted within his duties and with due diligence.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that while there were procedural breaches, they were rectified and did not result in financial losses attributable to Wong's actions.

Party_Details.Name: Wong Sung Boon

Facts.Text: Wong Sung Boon was appointed as Senior Managing Director and CEO of Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd.
Wong's appointment as Senior MD was governed by a Letter of Appointment dated 1 April 2016.
Wong was issued a termination notice summarily dismissing him from his position.
FXS suspended Wong with full pay pending the outcome of an audit investigation.
Deloitte issued a report with findings on the audit investigation.
FXS entered into a Master Services Agreement with I-Connect Interactive Pte Ltd for a satellite infrastructure project.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Wong Sung Boon contravened his duties leading to financial losses for Fuji Xerox Singapore Pte Ltd.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act with reasonable diligence and in the best interest of the company to avoid liability for financial losses.
Party_Details.Application: The Defendant by counterclaim argued that he acted within his duties and with due diligence. The Plaintiff by counterclaim contended that Wong's actions led to financial losses and exposure to liability.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court determined that while procedural breaches occurred, they were rectified and did not result in financial losses attributable to Wong's actions.
